pending.body: In `find_matching_running_task_run` (model.rs:467) and `matching_finalized_task_runs` (model.rs:485), the `_started_at: DateTime<Utc>` parameter is now completely unused (prefixed with underscore). These functions match solely by `run_id`. The parameter should be removed from the function signatures and all call sites (fs.rs:2821, fs.rs:2854) to avoid misleading callers into thinking `started_at` plays a role in matching. Leaving dead parameters in public functions creates a maintenance trap — future developers may assume the parameter is load-bearing and try to 'fix' bugs by adjusting the timestamp they pass.

The `collapse_task_run_attempts` pipeline (model.rs:499-573) retains full legacy runless matching: `find_collapse_group_index` (line 516-530) falls through to `unique_open_legacy_group_index` when `entry.run_id` is `None`, and `legacy_group_accepts_completion` (line 564-573) matches terminal entries to open legacy starts by `started_at`. While these are needed for backward-compatible reads of old data, the Issue 2 acceptance criteria states 'all runless matching/backfill code is removed.' Either (a) add a code comment block explaining that collapse is a read-only backward-compat path deliberately retained for legacy data, or (b) if the project truly has no legacy data, remove the functions. Without annotation, a future reviewer will flag the same gap.

In `update_task_run` (fs.rs:2960-3015), when the exact `run_id` match returns zero results (the `[]` arm at line 2961), the code falls through to search for open entries WITHOUT filtering by run_id (lines 2962-2968 filter only for non-terminal, non-superseded entries). If a single such open entry is found with a *different* run_id, the code returns an error (line 2999-3005) — but if zero open entries are found, it calls `unique_terminal_replay_match` (line 2982) which correctly requires run_id match. The intermediate fallback to `open_matches` without run_id filtering is confusing: for the `[index]` arm (single open entry with different run_id), the error message is generated but the path where `open_matches` has multiple entries (the `_` arm at line 3007) does not mention the run_id mismatch, producing a less helpful 'ambiguous' error. Consider adding the mismatched run_id(s) to the error message in the multi-open-match arm for debuggability.

Add a migration or one-time compatibility repair for persisted running task-run rows that still have `run_id: null`. `src/contexts/milestone_record/model.rs:462-477` and `src/adapters/fs.rs:2954-3005` now require an exact run-id match to finalize a row, so an in-flight legacy start from the previous build can no longer be completed after upgrade. Retrying start is not neutral either: `src/adapters/fs.rs:2893-2909` marks the legacy row failed and appends a new attempt. Without an upgrade step, mid-flight milestones from older versions will be stranded or misreported as failed.
